From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Canadian filmmaking trio

This article is about the filmmaking collective. For the airport with this code, see [Gimpo International Airport](/wiki/Gimpo_International_Airport "Gimpo International Airport").

RKSS

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Company type | Filmmaking collective |
| Founded | 2004; 22 years ago (2004) |
| Headquarters | [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), Canada |
| Key people | * Anouk Whissell * Yoann-Karl Whissell * François Simard (until 2024)[[1]](#cite_note-duo-1) |

**RKSS**, an initialism for **Roadkill Superstars**, is a filmmaking duo consisting of Canadian [film directors](/wiki/Film_director "Film director") **Anouk Whissell** and **Yoann-Karl Whissell**.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

They are known for writing and directing their debut feature film *[Turbo Kid](/wiki/Turbo_Kid "Turbo Kid")* (2015) and for directing the [horror](/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film") [mystery film](/wiki/Mystery_film "Mystery film") *[Summer of 84](/wiki/Summer_of_84 "Summer of 84")* (2018).

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=RKSS&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The group have been making short films since the mid-2000s, and cited their 2004 short *Le Bagman* as the first that "got an audience to follow [their] work", additionally crediting *Total Fury* (2007) and *Demonitron* (2010) as projects that further defined their style and technique.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

In 2011, the trio submitted a short to the anthology film *[The ABCs of Death](/wiki/The_ABCs_of_Death "The ABCs of Death")*, which features 26 short films based on each letter of the English alphabet. The film's producers [Ant Timpson](/wiki/Ant_Timpson "Ant Timpson") and [Tim League](/wiki/Tim_League "Tim League") held a competition seeking unknown directors to create a short for the letter "T", and the group submitted *T is for Turbo*, which placed third in the competition and was included on the film's home release.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Impressed with their work, Timpson approached them to develop the short into the feature film *Turbo Kid*.[[5]](#cite_note-5) [Jason Eisener](/wiki/Jason_Eisener "Jason Eisener"), who directed the *Y is for Youngbuck* section of the anthology, also supported RKSS at the [Fantasia International Film Festival](/wiki/Fantasia_International_Film_Festival "Fantasia International Film Festival")'s Frontieres Co-Production Market in 2013.[[6]](#cite_note-6) *Turbo Kid* premiered at the [2015 Sundance Film Festival](/wiki/2015_Sundance_Film_Festival "2015 Sundance Film Festival")[[7]](#cite_note-7) and was theatrically released on August 28, 2015, by [Epic Pictures Group](/wiki/Epic_Pictures_Group "Epic Pictures Group").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

RKSS signed to an agency after the success of *Turbo Kid*, and during a "tour of meetings" were approached by production company assistant Matt Leslie, who pitched them the idea for *[Summer of 84](/wiki/Summer_of_84 "Summer of 84")*.[[9]](#cite_note-The405-9) Leslie and co-writer Stephen J. Smith described the film's ending to the trio, and they signed on to direct.[[10]](#cite_note-Bloody-10) With the unexpected release and subsequent success of the [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix") series *[Stranger Things](/wiki/Stranger_Things "Stranger Things")* in 2016, the group was afraid of possible similarities between the two projects. Anouk Whissell watched the series and found it "very different", with only a reference to the [tabletop role-playing game](/wiki/Tabletop_role-playing_game "Tabletop role-playing game") *[Dungeons & Dragons](/wiki/Dungeons_%26_Dragons "Dungeons & Dragons")* removed from *Summer of 84* as a result.[[11]](#cite_note-11) The film premiered at the [2018 Sundance Film Festival](/wiki/2018_Sundance_Film_Festival "2018 Sundance Film Festival"), and received a [simultaneous release](/wiki/Simultaneous_release "Simultaneous release") in August 2018.[[12]](#cite_note-12) That October, it was released to streaming as a [Shudder](/wiki/Shudder_(streaming_service) "Shudder (streaming service)")-exclusive film.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

On November 1st 2024, François Simard was removed from the collective following criminal accusations.[[1]](#cite_note-duo-1)

### Future projects

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=RKSS&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Future projects")]

In January 2015, the group said that they were developing a revenge film titled *Elora*.[[14]](#cite_note-14) That August, they were preparing a pitch for that film, now referred to as *Elora's Death Wish*, along with an adaptation of the comic book series *[The Zombies That Ate the World](/wiki/The_Zombies_That_Ate_the_World "The Zombies That Ate the World")*.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

In September 2016, RKSS directed a music video for Le Matos' song "No Tomorrow", which also served as a prequel to *Turbo Kid* and featured [Laurence Leboeuf](/wiki/Laurence_Leboeuf "Laurence Leboeuf") reprising her role as Apple.[[16]](#cite_note-16) The next day, they confirmed that a sequel had been [greenlit](/wiki/Green-light "Green-light") and that they hoped to begin filming by mid-2017.[[17]](#cite_note-17) By August 2018, the group confirmed the film was still in development and that they were "deep in the writing process" for it.[[10]](#cite_note-Bloody-10) In February 2020, Timpson said that the script was finished and the crew was seeking financiers as the sequel requires "a little bit more money than [they] had for the first film".[[18]](#cite_note-18) In 2018, the group was also writing adaptations of two comics books, with Yoann-Karl Whissell saying that one is a [horror](/wiki/Horror_comics "Horror comics") comic and the other is about zombies.[[9]](#cite_note-The405-9)

## Filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=RKSS&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Filmography")]

| Year | Title | [Director](/wiki/Film_director "Film director") | [Writer](/wiki/Screenwriter "Screenwriter") | Role(s) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2015 | *[Turbo Kid](/wiki/Turbo_Kid "Turbo Kid")* | Yes | Yes | The Father (François Simard) The Mother (Anouk Whissell) Bald Guard (Yoann-Karl Whissell) |
| 2018 | *[Summer of 84](/wiki/Summer_of_84 "Summer of 84")* | Yes | No | —N/a |
| 2023 | *[We Are Zombies](/wiki/We_Are_Zombies "We Are Zombies")* | Yes | Yes |  |
| *Wake Up* | Yes | No |  |
